product.name: Horoscope App
product.tagline: A calm horoscope reader with offline fallbacks and native-ready shells.

hero.headline: A horoscope reader shaped around the daily ritual, not the scroll.
hero.subhead: Choose a sign, pick a focus, switch the mood, and keep the reading available whether it comes from a secure proxy, a demo endpoint, or a local generator.

summary: Horoscope App is a ritual-first reading surface built as a static web app and packaged with Capacitor shells for iOS and Android. The repo shows a clear product shape: short daily use, flexible content sources, and enough visual identity to feel like a real app rather than a one-off prototype.
audience: People who want a brief daily horoscope check-in rather than a noisy feed.
Users who like changing the visual tone of the app without losing the reading rhythm.
Makers testing a cross-platform astrology product without committing to one content provider.
coreProblems: Many horoscope apps turn the daily habit into a cluttered feed instead of a short ritual.
Live API dependence can leave a reading surface empty when a key or provider is unavailable.
Cross-platform packaging often splits the product into separate implementations instead of one consistent interface.

features.title: Daily, weekly, and monthly views
features.summary: The app surface already supports three reading ranges, so the ritual can stay brief or expand when needed.

features.title: Focus-aware readings
features.summary: General, love, career, money, and health selectors give the product a lightweight personalization layer.

features.title: Ten visual theme packs
features.summary: Named palettes shift the atmosphere of the app without changing the structure of the reading flow.

features.title: Five content modes
features.summary: Secure proxy, direct API key entry, public demo, placeholder copy, and local generation cover both live and fallback use.

features.title: Ritual extras
features.summary: Favorites, lucky color and number, compatibility, archive toggles, and energy bars are already present in the UI.

features.title: Native packaging path
features.summary: The same product world is packaged for iOS and Android through Capacitor shells stored in the repo.

workflows.title: Daily check-in
workflows.summary: Save a sign, keep the reading short, and rely on the one-line lead as the first layer of the ritual.

workflows.title: Offline fallback
workflows.summary: When no live provider is available, local generation and fallback copy keep the app useful instead of empty.

workflows.title: Phone install testing
workflows.summary: The web build can be synced into iOS and Android shells for private device testing without rebuilding the interface from scratch.

proofPoints.label: Signs
proofPoints.note: All zodiac signs are defined in the current selector and local generator.

proofPoints.label: Reading ranges
proofPoints.note: Daily, weekly, and monthly tabs are visible in the app shell.

proofPoints.label: Theme palettes
proofPoints.note: Ten named themes are defined in the current product surface.

proofPoints.label: Content modes
proofPoints.note: The repo exposes secure proxy, direct API, demo, placeholder, and local generation paths.

proofPoints.label: Platforms in repo
proofPoints.note: Web, iOS shell, and Android shell are all present in the current tree.

faq.question: Is this already published to an app store?
faq.answer: No app-store listing is evidenced in the repo. The safest description is a packaged prototype with native shells.

faq.question: Does the app need an API to work?
faq.answer: No. The repo includes placeholder copy and a local generator so the reading flow can still function without a live provider.

faq.question: What is the secure proxy for?
faq.answer: It keeps provider keys on the server side instead of storing them in the client runtime.

faq.question: What makes the interface distinct?
faq.answer: The repo emphasizes a short ritual: sign selection, focus selection, palette changes, a one-line lead, and visible energy bars.

screenshots.alt: Illustrated reading board showing horoscope card, focus controls, and energy bars
screenshots.caption: Illustrated board based on the current reading UI rather than a stored screenshot.

screenshots.alt: Illustrated poster using the crescent moon icon system from the repo
screenshots.caption: Poster image derived from the repo iconography and palette.

catalog.title: Horoscope App
catalog.blurb: A calm horoscope reader built for short daily check-ins, themed moods, and native-ready packaging.

repoArchetypeReasoning: The README describes a static horoscope app that runs in the browser and is packaged with Capacitor.
The feature surface lives in web/index.html and web/app.js, which makes the web build the primary implementation.
Native iOS and Android directories are present, so the public-facing product can credibly claim mobile-ready shells without claiming store release.
assumptions: No app-store availability is claimed because the repo does not evidence publication.
Support currently routes through GitHub issues because no dedicated support page exists in the repo.
The visual section uses illustrated assets because the repo does not include public screenshots.
missingButUsefulAssets: Actual product screenshots from web or device builds
A dedicated public privacy policy URL
A dedicated public support page outside the issue tracker
